dimensions.note: Docs-only slice plus one non-runtime JSDoc reword — no executable path touched. The newly documented ssoSession arg names the shared SSO session only; no token/secret documented. README uses placeholder identifiers throughout.
No logging code changed. The documented `aws sso login` remediation matches the shared ssoRemediation wording.

Behavior unchanged. The README now documents the full classified failure model already implemented: the `service` field, the `network` kind (checked first, transient DNS/socket), and the `preflight_sso` phase — all verified against _lib/scan_error.ts (ScanErrorSchema: service default "", kind enum network|auth_expired|access_denied|other) and _lib/aws_credentials.ts (PreflightSsoErrorFragment service:'sso'/phase:'preflight_sso').

No test surface changed. The full test suite (including the colocated manifest/model.version/upgrades-tail metadata test) passes after the version bump. The accountNameFromProfile behavior the corrected JSDoc now describes — "" only for an empty (ambient) profile, profile returned unchanged on a suffix non-match — is exercised by the existing suffix-handling unit tests.
Read-only fan-out scan (Describe* + GetCallerIdentity) unchanged and re-runnable; a docs/JSDoc edit cannot affect idempotency.

The published README contract now matches shipped code: scan_error service field lists sts/ec2/sso/"" (the exact literals at each throw site), kind is network|auth_expired|access_denied|other, and phases include preflight_sso alongside profile_suffix_check/credentials/describe_regions/describe_vpcs (verified against the phase literals in aws_vpc_inventory.ts). No SDK pins or request shapes changed.
No clients, handles, or files opened by a documentation/JSDoc change.

This IS the change: the README drifted behind the shared scan_error + SSO adoption and is corrected to document the `service` field, the `network` kind, the `preflight_sso` phase, and the `ssoSession` argument. The accountName JSDoc default was corrected to empty-string (no stripping) AND its result-value clause fixed: empty-string is returned ONLY for an ambient/empty profile; a profile that does not end with the configured suffix is returned UNCHANGED — verified against accountNameFromProfile (empty profile -> ""; empty suffix -> profile; non-match -> profile unchanged) and now consistent with that function's own adjacent JSDoc. `-readonly` is shown only as an example. Manifest and model.version aligned at 2026.07.19.0; wording mirrors the sibling @jentz/aws-rds-reservations README.

MECHANICAL: the README's scan_error field/enum documentation was diffed against ScanErrorSchema in _lib/scan_error.ts — 8 fields, service z.string().default("") (documented as reads-default-""), kind the closed z.enum. Schemas remain unchanged strict z.object; the edit only makes docs faithful to them.
vpc/scan_error keep lifetime 'infinite' and garbageCollection 10 — untouched by a docs change.
Read-only fan-out inventory; no create/update/delete lifecycle to complete. Unaffected by a documentation change.

The requiredProfileSuffix refusal and opt-in SSO pre-flight are unchanged in code; the README now documents the ssoSession pre-flight (empty default skips it), matching preflightSsoGate's ssoSession.length===0 early return.
No key construction changed. The documented vpc/scan_error keys reflect vpcKey and the canonical scanErrorKey(profile, region, service, phase).
No read/write data-access code changed; the downstream report contract (vpc/scan_error row shapes) is byte-identical. The README simply describes the service field accurately now.

Invariant holds: model.version bumped 2026.07.03.0 -> 2026.07.19.0 with a matching no-op upgrade entry appended (identity upgradeAttributes; description states docs-only, JSDoc default corrected, no resource schema changes). Chain tail equals model.version and manifest version, verified by the colocated metadata test in the passing suite.
